dividends of Rp12,000,123,702 (twelve billion one hundred twenty-three thousand seven hundred and two Rupiah) or Rp1 (one Rupiah) per share to shareholders from the performance results of the 2023 financial year. Laporan Tahunan / Annual Report 2024 26 PT Menthobi Karyatama Raya Tbk April 24 April Public Expose PT Menthobi Karyatama Raya Tbk Public Expose of PT Menthobi Karyatama Raya Tbk PT Menthobi Karyatama Raya Tbk menyelenggarakan Public Expose mengenai pemaparan terkait prospek dan kinerja bisnis Perseroan kepada masyarakat, para Investor dan pemangku kepentingan lainnya. PT Menthobi Karyatama Raya Tbk held a Public Expose to expose the Company’s business prospects and performance to the public, investors, and other stakeholders. April 24 April Inovasi dan Kebijakan Strategis yang Berkelanjutan dan Jangka Panjang Memperkuat Fundamental dan Kinerja MKTR di Tengah Potensi Tantangan Geopolitik Global dan La Nina Sustainable and Long-Term Strategic Innovation and Policies Strengthen MKTR’s Fundamentals and Performance Amidst Potential Global Geopolitical Challenges and La Nina Di tengah tantangan seperti La Nina dan meningkatnya tensi geopolitik global, MKTR optimistis tahun 2024 akan terjadi pertumbuhan yang lebih positif seiring dengan telah beroperasinya secara penuh fasilitas produksi baru dengan kontribusi yang lebih maksimal seperti pabrik KSO, pabrik pupuk organik, KCP, dan kebijakan strategis yang dilandasi semangat inovatif lainnya. Amid challenges such as La Nina and increasing global geopolitical tensions, MKTR is optimistic there will be more positive growth in 2024 as new production facilities are fully operational with maximum contributions such as KSO factories, organic fertilizer factories, KCP, and strategic policies based on other innovative spirits. Laporan Tahunan / Annual Report 2024 27 PT Menthobi Karyatama Raya Tbk Kilas Kinerja 2024 2024 Performance Overview Mei 12 May PT Menthobi Makmur Lestari Telah Berhasil Menyelesaikan Pembangunan Pabrik Kernel Crushing Plant PT Menthobi Makmur Lestari has Successfully Completed the Construction of the Kernel Crushing Plant PT Menthobi Makmur Lestari sebagai salah satu entitas anak dari PT Menthobi Karyatama Raya Tbk, telah berhasil menyelesaikan proses pembangunan serta melakukan commissioning pabrik Kernel Crushing Plant (KCP) dengan kapasitas 60 ton per hari. Beroperasinya pabrik KCP dapat memperkuat rantai nilai industri hilir khususnya dalam pemanfaatan inti sawit menjadi produk turunan yang memiliki nilai tambah tinggi. Investasi ini merupakan strategi jangka panjang MKTR dalam mendorong diversifikasi usaha dan integrasi vertikal yang dapat meningkatkan pertumbuhan bisnis yang berkelanjutan. PT Menthobi Makmur Lestari, one of the subsidiaries of PT Menthobi Karyatama Raya Tbk, has completed the construction process and commissioned the Kern

evidence.shareholders: shareholders. Any dividends received on the equity will be reinvested. BBDGF will only invest in equity instruments issued by companies from developed countries and will invest in at least 3 different economic sectors. The portfolio will be managed subject to the investment restrictions as defined in section 3.2 of the prospectus. BBDGF may enter into any investment, divestment or other business transaction with any other funds managed by the Fund Manager or with related party funds, on the terms and conditions as applicable under the relevant funds’ documentation with the prior approval of the Participants by Ordinary Consent. Valuations in relation to any such transaction may be performed by one or more parties (i.e. administrator) involved in such transaction as defined in 10.1 of the prospectus. The Board Members authorised these financial statements for issue on 26 August 2025. PANDION BBDGF, Nieuwegein | Semi-annual report 2025 9 General accounting policies General accounting principles The financial statements are prepared in accordance with Part 9, Book 2 of the Dutch Civil Code. The accounting principles of the Sub-fund are summarised below. These accounting principles have all been applied consistently throughout the reporting period. The semi-annual figures have not been audited by an independent auditor. Reporting period The reporting period covers the period from 1 January through 30 June 2025. Basis of accounting Valuation of assets and liabilities and determination of the result takes place under the historical cost convention. Unless presented otherwise at the relevant principle for the specific balance sheet item, assets and liabilities are valued according to the cost model. The financial statements of the Sub -fund have been prepared on a going concern basis as the management has no indications that the activities cannot be continued in the near future. Recognition of an asset or liability Assets and liabilities are stated at historical cost, unless stated otherwise in the other accounting policies. An asset is recognised in the balance sheet when it is probable that the future economic benefits will flow to the company and the asset has a cost or value that can be measured reliably. Assets that do not meet this requirement are not included in the balance sheet, but are classified as off-balance sheet assets. A liability is recognised in the balance sheet when it is probable that settlement thereof will involve an outflow of resources embodying economic benefits and the amount for which settlement will take place can be measured reliably. Obligations also include provisions. Liabilities that do not meet this requirement are not included in the balance sheet, but are accounted for as off-balance sheet commitments. An asset or liability recognised on the balance sheet remains on the balance sheet if a transaction does not lead to a significant change in the economic reality of the asset or liability. Such transactions also do not give rise to the recognition of results. The assessment of whether there has been a material change in economic reality is based on the economic benefits and risks that are most likely to materialise in practice and not on the basis of benefits and risks that cannot reasonably be expected to materialise will occur. An asset or liability is no longer included in the balance sheet if a transaction results in all or substantially all rights to economic benefits and all or virtually all risks relating to the asset or liability being transferred to a third party. In that case, the results of the transaction are recognised directly in the profit and loss account, taking into account any provisions that must be made in connection with the transaction. If the representation of economic reality leads to the inclusion of assets of which the legal person does not have legal ownership, this fact is stated. Offsetting an Asset and a Liability A financial asset and a financial liability are offset and reported as a net amount in the balance sheet when: - There is a legally enforceable right to offset the recognised amounts and settle them simultaneously; and - There is a firm intention to settle the net amount or both items simultaneously. PANDION BBDGF, Nieuwegein | Semi-annual report 2025 10 Recognition of Income and Expenses Income is recognised in the profit and loss account when an increase in economic potential, related to an increase in an asset or a decrease in a liability, has taken place, the amount of which can be measured reliably. Expenses are recognised when a reduction in the economic potential associated with a reduction in an asset or an increase in a liability has occurred, the amount of which can be reliably determined. Income and costs are allocated to the period to which they relate. Revenue is recognised when all significant risks
